name: Intelligent Transportation Systems

dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: The certificate pairs a data-systems foundation (CS 631, IS 531), spatial analysis (TRAN 602 GIS), traffic analysis (TRAN 615), and the capstone TRAN 755 (Intelligent Transportation Systems), a defensible data-plus-transportation theme; however CS 631 and IS 531 are two overlapping database courses in a ~4-course credential, and TRAN 755 requires TRAN 752 (Traffic Control), which is not on this menu, breaking the intended path.
dimensions.suggestion: Replace one of the two database courses with a transportation-specific data/analytics elective and add TRAN 752 (or TRAN 755's true prerequisite) to the menu so the capstone is reachable.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: deepestPrereqChain=1 with missingCourseCount=1: TRAN 698 is a dangling reference, and separately TRAN 755's stated prerequisite TRAN 752 is absent from the certificate list, so a student choosing only from the menu cannot satisfy the capstone's prerequisite.
dimensions.suggestion: Resolve the TRAN 698 dangling reference and include TRAN 752 (or waive it explicitly) for certificate students.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No program-level learning outcomes or objectives are present, so the certificate does not state which ITS competencies it certifies.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish 3-4 certificate learning outcomes describing the traffic-data, GIS, and ITS-operations skills a completer gains.

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: statedTotalCredits and creditReconcileDelta are null with no parsed plan, expected for a certificate; courses are uniformly 3 credits, but the two overlapping database courses (CS 631, IS 531) risk spending a quarter of a 12-credit credential on redundant content.
dimensions.suggestion: State the certificate total and avoid duplicated database coverage by requiring only one of CS 631 / IS 531.

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: All five sampled courses carry clear, specific descriptions (e.g., TRAN 615 traffic-stream characteristics and flow relationships; TRAN 755 incident detection and traffic management), and titles are legible.
dimensions.suggestion: Add a short certificate blurb explaining how the data-systems and transportation courses combine toward ITS competencies.

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: ITS is a current field, but this menu reflects an earlier, database-and-GIS-centric view; it omits the modern ITS core, connected/automated vehicles, V2X, real-time sensor/IoT data, traffic simulation, and machine-learning prediction, leaving TRAN 755 to carry currency alone.
dimensions.suggestion: Add a connected/automated-vehicles elective and a transportation-data-science/simulation elective to reflect current ITS practice.

descriptionQuality.justification: All five sampled courses carry specific, informative descriptions with no placeholders (e.g., CS 631 relational database design; TRAN 602 GIS spatial data).

prerequisiteClarity.justification: Prerequisites are stated (e.g., TRAN 615 elementary probability/statistics, CS 631 C and data structures), but TRAN 755 depends on TRAN 752 which is off the menu and TRAN 698 is a dangling reference (missingCourseCount=1), creating a real gap.

missingTopics.rationale: The menu has no coverage of vehicle-to-everything communication or automation, now the core of ITS.
TRAN 615 covers capacity analytically but no course teaches microsimulation (VISSIM/SUMO) essential to ITS design.

missingTopics.topic: Transportation data science and machine learning
missingTopics.rationale: Two general database courses (CS 631, IS 531) stand in for what should be predictive-analytics and sensor-data methods for traffic.

missingTopics.topic: Sensor systems and IoT for transportation
missingTopics.rationale: ITS is built on real-time detection; no course addresses sensors, edge computing, or data streams.
Connected infrastructure introduces security risks not covered anywhere in the menu.

emergingTrends.rationale: CAV deployment is reshaping ITS; TRAN 755 should evolve to include V2X and automation.
Streaming sensor data and ML prediction are replacing the static database approaches implied by CS 631/IS 531.
ITS increasingly integrates multimodal, app-based mobility the certificate could position for.
GIS (TRAN 602) and simulation are converging into digital-twin operations for corridors and cities.
Real-time control extends TRAN 752/TRAN 755 toward distributed sensing and computing.

topSuggestions: Fix the capstone path: add TRAN 752 (TRAN 755's prerequisite) to the menu and resolve the TRAN 698 dangling reference.
Replace one redundant database course (CS 631 or IS 531) with a transportation-data-science/analytics elective.
Modernize with connected/automated-vehicle and traffic-simulation electives.
Publish certificate learning outcomes and state total credits / course count.
Add a certificate blurb explaining how the data-systems and transportation courses combine.
